steps.stdout: Need an IPv4 address for example.com. Start a recursive lookup.
Browser sends one recursive query to its resolver: A example.com.
Cache miss: no unexpired A record exists, so walk the DNS hierarchy.
Query a root server. Root servers direct the resolver to the correct top-level domain.
Root referral received: ask a .com nameserver next. This is not the final IP.
Query the .com TLD nameserver to learn who is authoritative for example.com.
TLD referral received: ns.example.com owns this domain's DNS records.
Query the authoritative nameserver — the source of truth for example.com.
Authoritative answer: example.com A = 93.184.216.34, valid for 3600 seconds.
Store the answer in the resolver cache until its TTL expires.
Return 93.184.216.34 to the browser. Later requests can use the cached answer.
